---
title: "how ubuntu 10.10 connect eth0 permanent?"
date: 2010-10-30
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by cinkcink90 on 2010-10-30
i've tried to connect fresh installed ubuntu 10.10 to internet via LAN (eth0). ilm using configuration 
ifconfig eth0 add 192.168.1.20 netmask 255.255.255.0
route add default gw 192.168.1.1
nano /etc/resolv.conf

#Generated by NetworkManager
nameserver 203.130.196.155

the problem is. when i restart my ubuntu, i have to always do that configuration before i start the browser. 
could somebody help, how to automatically apply the configuration, so i can start the browser immediately ?

---

### Post by papibe on 2010-10-30
In the desktop version, a program called Network Manager overwrites any manual changes to the files you mention. You need you configure the network using a GUI interface.

Go to System -> Preferences -> Network Connections.

Good Luck!

---
